name: VectorCode
description: CLI/library that builds a local vector index of a repo for RAG-style code retrieval

implementation-language.comment: Core CLI/library is Python (Python 3.11-3.13). The bundled Neovim plugin is written in Lua, but the indexing/retrieval engine itself is Python.

status.comment: Pre-1.0 but functional; latest release 0.7.20 (2025-12-15). Not archived/discontinued as of 2026-07-09 check.

website.comment: GitHub repo serves as the primary landing page; a docs site (docs.vectorcode.davidyz.com) also exists but was not independently verifiable at check time.

form-factor.comment: Ships as a CLI, a Python library/API, an MCP server (vectorcode-mcp-server), a Neovim plugin, and optional git hooks (pre-commit/post-checkout) that auto-update the index.

standalone-usable.comment: General-purpose tool usable from any agent via its CLI or MCP server; not welded to a single host.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone tool, not bundled inside a specific agent/IDE.

install-mechanism.comment: Distributed on PyPI; recommended install via uv/pipx (pip ecosystem).

retrieval-technique.comment: Semantic vector retrieval over ChromaDB embeddings. Tree-sitter is used for chunking, not as a separate retrieval path, so not tagged hybrid.

query-interface.comment: Free-text query via the `query` command; also callable as MCP tool calls.

returns.comment: Returns matching document paths by default; a chunk-only output mode returns the retrieved code chunks.

structural-awareness.comment: Uses py-tree-sitter (tree-sitter-language-pack) for syntax-aware AST chunking on supported languages.

chunking-strategy.comment: Tree-sitter AST-aware chunking for detected languages (since 0.4.11); falls back to fixed-size string chunking (default 2500 chars) for unsupported languages.

reranking.comment: Offers NaiveReranker (vector-distance sort) and CrossEncoderReranker (sentence-transformer cross-encoder) as a second-stage reorder.

index-location.comment: ChromaDB index lives locally; a self-hosted remote Chroma server is optionally supported but that is still user-run, not a vendor cloud.

incremental-updates.comment: `update` subcommand refreshes only indexed files and removes entries for deleted files.

real-time-watch.comment: Optional git hooks (pre-commit, post-checkout) auto-update the index on commit/branch switch; not a continuous file watcher.

vector-store.comment: ChromaDB is the backing store (bundled local, optional standalone Chroma server).

embedding-model.comment: Defaults to a local SentenceTransformer model; any ChromaDB embedding function works (Ollama and sentence-transformers tested; OpenAI configurable).

mcp-support.comment: Ships a vectorcode-mcp-server; repo topics include mcp and mcp-server.

agent-compatibility.comment: Consumable by any MCP-capable agent via its MCP server; also integrates with Neovim's codecompanion.nvim.

ide-integrations.comment: First-class Neovim plugin (with LSP mode) plus the terminal CLI; no VS Code/JetBrains extension.

language-agnostic.comment: Indexes arbitrary text via string-chunking fallback; retrieval quality is better on tree-sitter-supported languages but any file can be embedded.

supported-languages.value: Any text-based file; AST chunking for languages in tree-sitter-language-pack, string-chunking fallback otherwise

code-leaves-machine.comment: Default configuration uses local SentenceTransformer embeddings and a local ChromaDB, so nothing leaves the machine. Egress only occurs if the user configures a hosted embedding provider (e.g. OpenAI).

local-only-option.comment: Fully offline path exists: local embedding model + bundled ChromaDB, no external API required.

self-hostable.comment: OSS tool that runs entirely on the user's own machine/infrastructure; optional standalone Chroma server is also self-hosted.

telemetry.comment: No telemetry collection by VectorCode itself is documented.

max-repo-scale.comment: Positioned as a per-project local RAG tool with an optional standalone server to reduce IO overhead; no monorepo-scale positioning. Judged medium; no explicit hard limit documented.

pricing-model.comment: Free OSS run locally; BYOK applies if you opt into a paid hosted embedding provider.

maturity.comment: Usable and actively developed through 2025 with frequent releases, but pre-1.0 (0.7.x), single-maintainer, and Neovim-centric; release cadence slowed after Dec 2025.

active-maintenance.comment: Latest visible commit 2025-12-26 and latest release 0.7.20 (2025-12-15) are both >90 days before the 2026-07-09 check date, so outside the ~90-day active-maintenance window.

community-size.comment: ~871 GitHub stars, single maintainer, primarily a Neovim/MCP niche audience.
